mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC for UNRATE > 5.5% remains above 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined index of INDPRO and HOUST declines shows no statistically significant negative correlation with 20-day SPX returns (p > 0.10), or forward returns are positive in > 50% of contraction episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising real credit conditions tighten financial constraints on leveraged firms, suppressing equity valuations over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline when RSAFS growth slows alongside FEDFUNDS > 3.0%, or the conditional IC remains positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly scales corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings revisions cascade through equity pricing.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for GDPC1 growth remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in positive GDP growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 115, it signals capacity utilization stress that triggers deflationary expectations and margin compression, predicting negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances where INDPRO drops below 115, or the predictive power vanishes after controlling for UNRATE movements.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in unemployment and persistence of elevated federal funds rates creates a stagflationary feedback loop that crushes equity risk premiums, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint distribution of UNRATE and FEDFUNDS shows no statistically significant negative correlation with 5-day SPX returns (p > 0.05), or forward returns are not systematically negative when both series move adversely.

mechanism_narrative: During housing market expansions, rising mortgage credit availability amplifies consumer spending, but when federal funds rates exceed 4.0%, the rate hike cycle abruptly halts this demand transmission, causing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline when FEDFUNDS crosses 4.0% alongside RSAFS deceleration, or the interaction term in a regression of 20d returns on RSAFS and FEDFUNDS is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).
